title: TaTe's Structure Reworks
description: Updates Structures in a Variety of Ways, Improving Their Aesthetics, Loot, and Overall Experience
body:
![Preview](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8fcc69011ea68a65bdb7e3f5bcac201e18e8a0df.png)

# **IMPORTANT** - I (AverageTaTeMan) only share my content on [Planet Minecraft](https://www.planetminecraft.com/member/averagetateman/), [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/user/AverageTaTeMan), and [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/members/averagetateman/projects) - If you see this project on any other site, IT IS NOT ME AND SHOULD NOT BE TRUSTED!

This pack aims to enhance/improve the existing structures. Whether it be bringing the appearance up to modern standards or buffing underwhelming loot tables, this pack has you covered!

The following structures have been tweaked, meaning they'll look and work mostly the same, but there's a few noticable differences:
- Trail Ruins
- Trial Chambers
- End City
- Woodland Mansion
- Bastion
- Ancient City
- Pillager Outpost

The following structures have been remade from the ground up, massively improving the appearance, loot, and overall experience:
- Stronghold
- Nether Fortress
- Ocean Monument
- Dungeon (Original Variant Still Exists)

Many of the structures now also feature Vaults like Trial Chambers, each with a unique texture (CTM mod required). This allows multiple players to reap valuable rewards from the same structure as long as they have the appropriate key:
- Trail Ruins
- End City
- Woodland Mansion
- Bastion
- Ancient City
- Stronghold
- Nether Fortress
- Ocean Monument

Keys for the following structures have been added (some structures currently don't have Vaults for their keys to be used on, they will be added later on):
- Trail Ruins
- End City
- Woodland Mansion
- Bastion
- Ancient City
- Stronghold
- Nether Fortress
- Ocean Monument
- Dungeon
- Desert Pyramid
- Jungle Temple
- Igloo
- Witch Hut
- Pillager Outpost
- Shipwreck

Keys can be obtained in the following ways:
- The Wandering Trader will always have a key available at the end of its trades
- Killing various notable mobs
- Defeating Trial Spawners inside a structure.
- Various loot tables across the world


Gazing:
- New mini-boss with 100 hearts that resides above the central Spawner in the new Dungeon.
- Afflicts Mining Fatigue to players in the Dungeon.
- The Gazing strongly dislikes explosives, blinking away nearby explosive blocks, and petrifying most explosive entities, causing them to slowly crumble before eventually vanishing.  
- Attacks players by shooting a laser from its eye, like a Guardian, and bites those who dare to get too close.
- When a player that has Bad/Trial Omen comes nearby, the Gazing upgrades to an Ominous Gazing, which has double the health and afflicts stronger effects.
- Pickaxes do double the damage.
- When killed, drops Gazing Remains, which can do three things:
	1) Can be worn on the head slot.
	2) Can be eaten to clear Mining Fatigue, with a chance to add additional positive/negative effects.
	3) Can be placed down to revive the Gazing, except it no longer afflicts negative effects.
- Despawns in Peaceful

New Spear Enchantment: - Cavalry Surge:
- Use the Spear while riding mobs to boost their speed!
- On Boats and Minecarts, they’re instead boosted forward!
- Can’t be used too much, or will go on cooldown for a short bit.  A warning when the ability will go on cooldown if use is continued is given a few seconds ahead of time.
- Two Levels, Level II is faster, but causes the cooldown quicker.
- Incompatible with Lunge


Almost every structure's loot table has been overhauled, with the changes listed below only scratching the surface...

Structures have higher chances for certain Enchanted Books:
- **Ancient City** - Mending
- **Desert Pyramid/Jungle Temple** - Unbreaking
- **Stronghold** - Protection, Blast Protection, Projectile Protection, Feather Falling
- **Ocean Monument** - Impaling, Riptide, Loyalty, Channeling
- **Abandoned Mineshaft** - Efficiency, Fortune, Silk Touch
- **Buried Treasure** - Depth Strider, Frost Walker, Respiration, Aqua Affinity
- **Nether Fortress** - Fire Protection, Fire Aspect, Flame
- **Dungeon** - Sharpness, Smite, Bane of Arthropods, Power
- **Pillager Outpost** - Quick Charge, Piercing, Multishot
- **Woodland Mansion** - Lunge, Cavalry Surge

All the Music Discs are now associated with a single structure (the Creeper-Skeleton method remains):
- **Dungeon** - Cat, 13
- **Pillager Outpost** - Chirp
- **Woodland Mansion** - Lava Chicken, Stal
- **Ancient City** - 5 (Fragments), Ward
- **Abandoned Mineshaft** - 11, Bounce (Only in Sulfur Caves)
- **Stronghold** - Otherside, Far
- **Desert Pyramid** - Blocks
- **Jungle Temple** - Mall, Strad
- **Buried Treasure** - Wait, Mellohi
- **Trail Ruins** - Relic
- **Trial Chambers** - Creator, Creator (Music Box), Precipice
- **Bastion** - Pigstep
- **Nether Fortress** - Tears


**Installation Instructions to Get the Data AND Resource Packs:**
1) Click "Versions"
![Click "Versions"](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0fa5986659f549bce8d54fc7fa0182ce7c9cee0f.png)

2) Select a Version
![Select a Version](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/786593f6790747dfe34d64f1f31ba6fe90646678.png)

3) Under "Files", click "Download" on both the 'Primary' file (data pack) and 'Required resource pack' file (resource pack)
![Under "Files", click "Download" on both the 'Primary' file (data pack) and 'Required resource pack' file (resource pack)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9dbfcbe10de4a5520daa540e68d03397f930291f.png)

4) Unzip the files (optional, but recommended as that's how I use/test them). 

5) Add the resource pack to your Selected Packs (ideally at the top) in your Resource Packs menu. 

6) Add the data pack to either your Selected Packs in the Data Packs menu (if adding at world creation) OR put it in your world's 'datapacks' folder (if adding afterwards.  Recommended to either not have world open at the time or to close and reopen it afterwards).

7) You may get a prompt about experimental settings not being supported when opening the world.  This can safely be ignored, but it's also never a bad idea to make a backup if it's a world you care about.
